Title: Functional mechanism study of the allelochemical myrigalone A identifies a group of ultrapotent inhibitors of ethylene biosynthesis in plants

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Dictyostelium discoideum; Arabidopsis thaliana; Petunia × hybrida
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Dictyostelium discoideum; Arabidopsis thaliana; Petunia × hybrida
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes seed germination, regulates root/hypocotyl growth, root hair formation, development and senescence
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Headspace of starving Dictyostelium cultures; headspace of Arabidopsis seedling vials
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Described as catalyzing the final and rate-limiting step of ethylene synthesis in plants
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes ACC to produce ethylene in the final step of the ethylene biosynthetic pathway
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: In D. discoideum, Aco expression increased after 8 h starvation and peaked at 12 h during development
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Starvation during Dictyostelium development; Arabidopsis seed germination and seedling assays under light/low-light; treatments with MyA, AIB, POA, ACC, CEPA, 1-MCP
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Unbiased REMI-seq chemical-genetic screen; CRISPR knockout/ablation; developmental phenocopy analysis; exogenous ethylene rescue with CEPA; metabolite profiling of ethylene by GC/MS or ETD-300; in silico docking/modeling; pharmacological comparison with AIB and POA
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Next-generation sequencing/REMI-seq mutant abundance profiling; no accession provided
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ACO activity promotes ethylene production; genetic loss or chemical inhibition of ACO reduces ethylene production, delays Dictyostelium development, delays Arabidopsis seed germination, suppresses root and hypocotyl growth, and blocks ethylene-dependent root hair and hook responses
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ACO was identified as the main MyA-associated target in a genome-wide D. discoideum resistance screen. 2. CRISPR ablation of Aco phenocopied MyA treatment and reduced sensitivity to MyA. 3. MyA and Aco loss both reduced ethylene production, and exogenous ethylene partially rescued developmental defects. 4. Structural modeling placed MyA, AIB, and POA in the catalytic pocket of ACO. 5. In Arabidopsis, MyA reproduced known ACO-inhibitor phenotypes and reduced ethylene production, supporting conserved ACO inhibition.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: ACO is a strong target for chemical or genetic manipulation of ethylene-dependent traits such as seed dormancy/germination, seedling architecture, senescence, postharvest lifespan, stress responses, and grain quality; caution that excessive ethylene suppression may impair normal growth and development
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced germination and seedling growth; developmental delay; possible broader off-target or secondary effects cannot be excluded
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper’s causal focus is inhibition of ACO protein activity by allelochemicals/small molecules rather than transgenic engineering of ACO. Direct biochemical binding was inferred by docking and strong phenocopy/rescue evidence, but not shown by purified plant ACO binding assay in vitro. Source species and applied species differ across orthologs and assay systems.
